question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: mRNA leaves the nucleus to go and be checked for errors. Once the mRNA is in the cytoplasm, protein syntheis follows along the line of nitrogen bases to check for mutation or mistakes. The mistakes are found and labeled to be change, then an enzyme comes to re-do the mistaken nitrogen bases.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: 1. the mRNA leaves the nucleus and searches for a ribosome2. the mRNA finds a ribosome and attaches to it3. the mRNA is decoded by tRNA via base pairing rules and the amino acids, previously synthesized, are brought in as indicated by the mRNA.4. the ribosome moves on down the mRNA strand, assembling the amino acids in the correct order.

Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane are osmosis, facilitated diffusion and active transport. Osmosis is the diffusion of water across a semi-permiable membrane. Facilitated diffusion is the diffusion of a large molecule across a semi-permiable membrane that requires help, such as transport protiens. Active diffusion is the diffusion of a molecule that requires the use of energy.

question: Invasive Species item: The article discusses nonnative reptiles, especially Burmese pythons in Florida, and presents two views: MacInnes argues that the term 'invasive' is unfair and judgmental, while biologists argue that invasive species can spread without natural predators and threaten biodiversity. Explain the significance of the word 'invasive' to the rest of the article. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the word 'invasive' is significant because it frames nonnative species as harmful, spreading, and disruptive rather than merely introduced. In the article, pythons and other reptiles are described as threats to biodiversity because they can spread without natural predators and endanger native species. A strong response may also recognize the contrast between MacInnes's view that the word is judgmental and the biologists' view that invasive species are a serious ecological threat. Scoring guide: score 2 = thoughtful and thorough examination of the text with relevant support; score 1 = sufficient but limited examination with some relevant support; score 0 = cursory, vague, unsupported, inaccurate, or irrelevant response.
student_answer: In the article it is said in two ways at first it says that saying "invasive species" is unfair because they are introduced it's past judgement. Then on the other hand invasive species, unchecked by natural predators are major threats to biodiversity.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: a) Based on the student's data, plastic B stretched the most by 22-23 mm and plastic A stretched the least with about 11mm. Plastic had the second strongest stretchability with 20mm and then plastic with 13-14 mm.  b) The students could have improved the experiment by recording the average amount stretched. They also could have made sure all the plastics had same beginning length.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: prophasemetaphasetelophaseanaphase

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: One way is by using Cilia to move substances across the membrane with small hair-like apendages. Another way is to use Flagella long whip-like apendages used to move substances across the cell membrane. One more way to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane is by releasing ribosomes.

reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: Growth, expand, divide, new.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: when the mRNA leaves the nucleus, it connects with proteins, and then it coneects with high energy and it goes around the cell. After that it connects with amino acids.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: protects the cell

question: Invasive Species item: The article discusses nonnative reptiles, especially Burmese pythons in Florida, and presents two views: MacInnes argues that the term 'invasive' is unfair and judgmental, while biologists argue that invasive species can spread without natural predators and threaten biodiversity. Explain the significance of the word 'invasive' to the rest of the article. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the word 'invasive' is significant because it frames nonnative species as harmful, spreading, and disruptive rather than merely introduced. In the article, pythons and other reptiles are described as threats to biodiversity because they can spread without natural predators and endanger native species. A strong response may also recognize the contrast between MacInnes's view that the word is judgmental and the biologists' view that invasive species are a serious ecological threat. Scoring guide: score 2 = thoughtful and thorough examination of the text with relevant support; score 1 = sufficient but limited examination with some relevant support; score 0 = cursory, vague, unsupported, inaccurate, or irrelevant response.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: For both trials they had similar results. They could have improved their experiment by adding more weights instead of keeping them the same.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Cell respiration, cell movement, and cellular activation

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Three process used by the cell to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane are active transport, passive transport, and a semi-permable membrane.  Active transport is the process in which the cell moves materials across its membrane with the use of energy.  Passive transport is the movement of material across the cell membrane without the use of energy.  A semi-permable membrane is what helps the cell control what pass through the cell and how it gets in.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. student_answer: When the mRNA leaves the nucleous, it is taking a copy of the DNA to make more copies and create a protein. As it leaves, it goes into the ribosome where the protein synthesis and copying of the DNA actually happens. In the ribosome, amino acids with the corresponding letters match up to the new copy of the DNA. Stop codons are added to the end and a protein is now made.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: After mRNA leaves the nucleus, it attaches to a ribosome. The mRNA contains a codon that will bind with a specific anticodon. Transfer RNA carries that specific anticodon to the codon on the mRNA. The codon and anticodon bind, and form an amino acid. More and more tRNA molecules bring anticodons to the mRNA codons, which produce a growing polypeptide chain. This chain will be a protein.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mRNA leaves the nucleus.  The cells travel to a ribosome to make proteins.  Then, the proteins travel to the mitochondia to make energy.  Then, the cell leaves the mitochondia and makes it way to the amino acids.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mRNA will reach a ribosome. The tRNA will find the amino acid that the mRNA needs. The amino acids will stick together. A protein will be made out of the amino acid strand.

It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.

They are different from each other because pythons are more likely to be harmful rather than pandas and koalas. Pythons can eat just about anything while koalas and pandas eat nothing.

Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: The cells movement is controlled by the cell membrane. It controls what comes into the cell and what leaves the cell. Although the nucleus controls the cell and tells it to move the membrane does most of the work.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: First, mRNA takes the codes to the ribosome to make the proteins. Then, the rRNA codes for the proteins and makes them. Then the tRNA transfers the proteins back to the nucleus. Finally, the proteins are released into the cytoplasm, and the process begins again.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Active Transport is one of the three. In active transport, the cell has to use energy to collect substances and pass through the membrane. This energy is needed due to the flow of substances outside the cell. The next process is passive transport. In passive transport, the cell does not need any force exerted to gather and allow substances with in the cell. This is because the concentration is much higher within the cell. The final process is osmosis, which is simply substances of higher concentration flowing into an area of lower concentration due to mixing, heat, or friction.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: A conclusion that can be drawn from the students' polymer investigation would be that plastic type A was the strongest plastic used. It took the least amount of stretches although it had gained two stretches on the second trial. Two ways the students could have improved their experiment are that they could have increase the amount of plastic types and include more trials of testing the different plastics.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: First, the mRNA leaves the nucleus to go to the DNA. Then it copies one strand of the DNA. Then it makes ribosomes to translate it. The codons get turned into amino acid and ribosomes attatch to groups of 3 amino acids to translate them. After they are done, they release and continue.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Osmosis is one way cells move water across its membrane. Osmosis is the diffusion of water. Diffusion is when molecules move from a high concentration to a low concentration across the cell membrane. Another way is by facilitated diffusion. Facilitated diffusion is when the molecules move from a low concentration to a high concentration, against the concentration gradient. This type of diffusion requires ATP. A third way molecules cross the cell membrane is by exocytosis. Exocytosis is when a cell releases waste out of the cell. This process occurs by diffusion.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Conclusion; plastics B+D stretch significantly more than plastics A+C. The students could have improved their design by starting with plastics of all the same length. They also should  named a common value for all the weights.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: a) The students concluded that platic B was the most flexible, stretching up to 23mm on the second trial and 22mm on the first. b) The student could have improved this design and validity of his lab in two ways. First he could have done the experiment a third time to further confirm results. Second, the student could have been more descriptive in his procedure explaing how to add weight to the clamp and how much weight to actually add.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: the cell crosses over the membrane

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: In conclusion. Plastic A was the least streched after two tries also plastic B was the most stretchest after the two tries. The student could have improved the experimental design by being more specific and also measured the length of the materials before doing the experiment.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Cells use different processes to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane. One of the processes is a semi permiable membrane that allows only certain substances to pass through. This membrane is like a pasta strainer. The strainer lets the water out and keeps the pasta in. another way cells control movement is by ATP. ATP is large amounts of cellular energy that is used for movement. Another way is by a cell wall. Cell walls do not allow any substance to pass in or out of the cell. There are many more ways a cell controls the movement of substances across the membrane, but these three are the most common.

reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: There are many ways a cells materials can enter of leave a cell. Endocytosis is how a cell takes in needed materials in which are to big to fit through the lipid bilayer. Exocytosis is a process in which cells get rid of any waste materials to big to fit through the lipid bilayer. Another way to move substances across the cell membrane is by the sodium-potassium pump. The sodium-potassium pump alows potassium to enter a cell, and sodium to leave a cell. Sodium-potassium pumps only open one way at a time. For example, when the pump is recieving the potassium from outside the cell, the sodium waiting on the indide of the cell can't move through. Endocytosis, Exocytosis, and sodium-potassium pump are three ways substances move across the cell membrane.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: The first process that cells use is diffusion. Cells have membranes that control what makes it into the cell and what cannot get inside the cell. The second process cells use are tunnel-like objects. Certain tunnels only can carry certain substances across the membrane and into the cell, such as a tunnel only being used for carbon. The third process cells use is called osmosis. Osmosis is the diffusion of water across a semi-permeable membrane. Water is a very simple and easy to carry molecule, so it easily diffuses, unless there is a balance of water on the inside and outside of the cell-equilibrium.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mRNA enters the ribosome. It then begins translation when the anticodons from the tRNA match up with the codons on the mRNA. The starting codon is always Met. As the tRNA attaches to the mRNA, the amino acids above them form peptide bonds. Finally, the last step is the stop codon on the mRNA. This signals the end of protein synthesis.

reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 2 = one creditable character trait of Rose and one adequate supporting story detail related to her conversations with Anna or Aunt Kolab; score 1 = one creditable character trait of Rose without adequate supporting detail; score 0 = no creditable character trait of Rose. question: Mr. Leonard item: In the story, Paul learns background information about Mr. Leonard: Mr. Leonard had been a talented college hurdler who won the 400-meter hurdles at nationals, but he struggled academically, lost his scholarship, and left college because he could not read well. Explain the effect this background information has on Paul. Support your response with details from the story.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the background information about Mr. Leonard affects Paul by helping him understand that Mr. Leonard has faced struggles similar to his own reading difficulties. Paul realizes that Mr. Leonard helped him become a better runner despite Mr. Leonard's own painful experience with school and lost opportunity. This makes Paul feel empathy and motivates him to help Mr. Leonard in return, shown when Paul says it is time to start Mr. Leonard's training. Scoring guide: score 2 = clear explanation of the effect on Paul with relevant story details; score 1 = partial or general explanation with limited, overly simple, or weakly supported details; score 0 = inaccurate, incomplete, missing, unsupported, or irrelevant response.
student_answer: Paul learned that Mr. Leonard was a track star who also couldn't read. His inability to read made life a lot more difficult for him; more difficult than it was for all of the other people that had gone to school with him. Paul, who also couldn't read, felt close to Mr. Leonard. He felt empathetic; he understood the hall monitor's emotions completely. He could identify with the embarrassment he felt.     Mr. Leonard had given Paul hope; he had shown him about hurdles and taught him how to jump over them efficiently and effectively. Eventually, Paul was able to join the track team. It had given him the chance to excel at something like never before. After hearing Mr. Leonard's story, he felt as though he should give back. After the story ends, it's clear that Paul is helping to teach Mr. Leonard how to read so that he too could feel accomplished with himself.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.
student_answer: The article states that both pandas in china and koalas in Africa are similar. They are both specialists. However, the article also states that pythons are generalist and can pretty much live anywhere in the world. Pandas and koalas need specific food and a specific climate in order to survive.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: mRNA delivers the plans for the construction of RNA. tRNA brings the various materials for the construction of RNA. rRNA produces RNA. RNA goes out of the ribosome.

He provides an exapmle of when two satellites, one Russian and one American, collided in space and created hundreds of pieces that now helplessly oribit earth. He does this to prove that even the smallest fo space junk can pose a threat to space ships entering space.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: The cell membrane has holes in  it for proteins and enzymes to pass through. It also has a gate to keep unwanted molecules out. Certain molecules must become attached to the passage for the molecule to pass through. Also the cellmembrane itself does not allow  molecules to go through it unless they have the right structure for it to go through.They must diffuse across the membrane.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: a) Based on the student data, plastic B is the most stretchable. Plastic B stretched an average of 22.5mm, while the next most stretched plastic was D, with an average of 20mm. ^p b) To improve validity/design, the student could have conducted more trials. The students also should have specified exactly how heavy the weights used are, so that the experiment is recreatable.

The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Plastic type D is the most durable. It had the most amount stretched. The student should have wrote in the measurements of the before stretched length and how many weights to add when stretched.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.
student_answer: Pandas in China are similar to koalas in Australia China's pandas eat nothing but bamboo. Australia's koala bear eats eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. They both are specialist and the python are different because its a generalist which is change and specialist is stability.

Identify one character trait that describes Rose based on her conversations with Anna or Aunt Kolab, and include one story detail that supports the trait.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 2 = one creditable character trait of Rose and one adequate supporting story detail related to her conversations with Anna or Aunt Kolab; score 1 = one creditable character trait of Rose without adequate supporting detail; score 0 = no creditable character trait of Rose. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Osmosis is where water comes in and this process has to keep cells for becoming too engorged. Diffusion- where molecules move through the cell membrane. Facilitated diffusion- that is where molecules cannot get through and need help to do so.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mitochondian breaks down the protiens. Then it is filtered through the body and into the blood. The protiens are used to supply energy to the body.

reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the word 'invasive' is significant because it frames nonnative species as harmful, spreading, and disruptive rather than merely introduced. question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: When mRNA leaves the Nucleus it goes through PMAT1. Prophase2. Metaphase3. Anaphase4. Telephase

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species.
